portrait: He carries loneliness like a cardigan, soft and familiar, transforming sharp edges into gentle folds. Behind the patience everyone sees lies a boy at the piano, playing minor keys to a question he's learning not to answer.

sections.howYouMove: Deliberately, with the weight of someone who has learned that rushing past feelings only makes them louder. Your hands find edges of things—cardigans, doorframes, piano keys—as if touch grounds you in the present moment.
sections.howYouThink: In progressions and refrains, circling back to the same questions like fingers finding familiar chords. You transform experience into purpose almost automatically, though you're beginning to notice the cost of always making pain useful.
sections.howYouConnect: Through witness and reflection, offering others what your grandfather gave you—love without conditions. Yet you struggle to receive that same unconditional presence, always earning your place through patience and purpose.
sections.whatYouCarry: The weight of that lonely boy who was 'too heavy, too much in his own head,' and the fear that without transforming his pain into goodness, he might not be enough. Your grandfather's absence echoes in every child you try to reach.
sections.whatLightsYouUp: The moment when music shifts from question to conversation, when minor keys find their way to genuine gentleness. Being truly seen without needing to be useful—though this light is so new it still startles you.
sections.yourContradictions: You teach children to sit with their feelings while running from your own grief. You've built a life on transforming loneliness into connection, yet fear that if the boy inside could just be—without earning his place—everything might unravel.
sections.yourVoice: Measured and musical, with pauses that hold as much meaning as words. You speak in gentle spirals, approaching truth sideways, your voice growing quieter as you near the tender places.

crystallizedMoments.quote: I'm playing for him as much as anything else.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: The moment you recognized you've been in conversation with your younger self all along.

crystallizedMoments.quote: Maybe I've been running from that boy's grief this whole time. Turning it into purpose so I wouldn't have to feel how much I missed him.
crystallizedMoments.reflection: The breakthrough realization that purpose can be a form of avoidance.
The shift from seeing your younger self as work to be done to company to keep.
